req.title: Recurring transfer
req.statement: A premium-tier customer shall be able to schedule a recurring transfer up to $10,000 per occurrence; non-premium accounts capped at $2,500.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-005 defines tier-based limits on recurring transfer scheduling. Test design targets: (1) equivalence partitioning of customer tiers (premium vs. standard), (2) boundary value analysis on per-occurrence limits ($10,000 premium / $2,500 standard), (3) negative path coverage for limit violations and unsupported frequencies, (4) decision table for tier × amount × frequency combinations. All tests are deterministic, self-contained, and verify schedule persistence and next-run calculation as required.

tests.name: test_premium_tier_recurring_transfer_at_limit
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with amount=$10000, frequency=MONTHLY, recipient_account=valid_iban, start_date=future_date
expected: Response body contains next_run_date calculated as start_date + 1 month
Schedule persisted in database (verified via GET /transfers/recurring/{schedule_id})
trace: R-FIN-005: expected outcome: schedule persisted; next run calculated
tests.executable:
import requests
import json
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_premium_tier_recurring_transfer_at_limit():
    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <premium_token>"}
    start_date = (datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=5)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": 10000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "recipient_account": "DE89370400440532013000",
        "start_date": start_date,
        "description": "Premium recurring transfer test"
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://banking-api/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}"
    data = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in data and len(data["schedule_id"]) == 36
    assert "next_run_date" in data
    verify_resp = requests.get(
        f"http://banking-api/transfers/recurring/{data['schedule_id']}",
        headers=headers
    )
    assert verify_resp.status_code == 200

tests.name: test_standard_tier_recurring_transfer_at_limit
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with amount=$2500, frequency=WEEKLY, recipient_account=valid_iban, start_date=future_date
expected: Response body contains next_run_date calculated as start_date + 1 week
trace: R-FIN-005: expected outcome: schedule persisted; next run calculated
tests.executable:
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_standard_tier_recurring_transfer_at_limit():
    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <standard_token>"}
    start_date = (datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=3)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": 2500,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "recipient_account": "GB82WEST12345698765432",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://banking-api/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in data
    assert "next_run_date" in data
    assert data["frequency"] == "WEEKLY"

tests.name: test_premium_tier_exceeds_per_occurrence_limit
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with amount=$10001, frequency=MONTHLY, recipient_account=valid_iban, start_date=future_date
expected: Response body contains message referencing premium limit of $10,000
trace: R-FIN-005: error pathway: 422 when per-occurrence limit exceeded
tests.executable:
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_premium_tier_exceeds_per_occurrence_limit():
    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <premium_token>"}
    start_date = (datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=5)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": 10001,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "recipient_account": "DE89370400440532013000",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://banking-api/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}"
    data = resp.json()
    assert "error_code" in data and data["error_code"] == "LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
    assert "10000" in str(data.get("message", ""))

tests.name: test_standard_tier_exceeds_per_occurrence_limit
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with amount=$2501, frequency=WEEKLY, recipient_account=valid_iban, start_date=future_date
expected: Response body contains message referencing standard limit of $2,500
trace: R-FIN-005: error pathway: 422 when per-occurrence limit exceeded
tests.executable:
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_standard_tier_exceeds_per_occurrence_limit():
    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <standard_token>"}
    start_date = (datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=3)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": 2501,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "WEEKLY",
        "recipient_account": "GB82WEST12345698765432",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://banking-api/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    data = resp.json()
    assert data["error_code"] == "LIMIT_EXCEEDED"
    assert "2500" in str(data.get("message", ""))

tests.name: test_unsupported_frequency_rejected
preconditions: Frequency value not in {DAILY, WEEKLY, BIWEEKLY, MONTHLY, QUARTERLY, ANNUAL}
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with amount=$5000, frequency=FORTNIGHTLY (unsupported), recipient_account=valid_iban, start_date=future_date
trace: R-FIN-005: error pathway: 422 when frequency unsupported
R-FIN-005: FORTNIGHTLY is not in supported frequency set
tests.executable:
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_unsupported_frequency_rejected():
    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <premium_token>"}
    start_date = (datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=5)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": 5000,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "FORTNIGHTLY",
        "recipient_account": "DE89370400440532013000",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://banking-api/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    data = resp.json()
    assert "error_code" in data and data["error_code"] == "INVALID_FREQUENCY"

tests.name: test_premium_tier_boundary_one_cent_below_limit
actions: POST /transfers/recurring with amount=$9999.99, frequency=MONTHLY, recipient_account=valid_iban, start_date=future_date
trace: R-FIN-005: boundary test: premium limit is $10,000
tests.executable:
import requests
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

def test_premium_tier_boundary_one_cent_below_limit():
    headers = {"Authorization": "Bearer <premium_token>"}
    start_date = (datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=5)).isoformat()
    payload = {
        "amount": 9999.99,
        "currency": "USD",
        "frequency": "MONTHLY",
        "recipient_account": "DE89370400440532013000",
        "start_date": start_date
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://banking-api/transfers/recurring",
        json=payload,
        headers=headers
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    data = resp.json()
    assert "schedule_id" in data
